name: Rye Yeast Bread
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Rye flour plus up to 1/2 cup
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm the water and grape juice to about 115°F and stir in the yeast. Let the mixture stand for about 10 minutes or until it is foamy.
2. Stir in the salt, oil, and 2 cups of the flour, and beat with a spoon or mixer until smooth.
3. Add enough of the remaining flour to make a soft dough. Knead on a floured board for 10 minutes.
4. Place dough in an oiled bowl, turn it over so the top is also oiled, and cover with a towel. Let rise in a warm place (85-90°F) for about 45 minutes, or until doubled in volume.
5. Shape the dough into a loaf and place in an oiled 8 x 4 inch loaf pan. Let rise again in a warm place for about 30 minutes, or until doubled in volume.
6. Bake at 425°F for 10 minutes, then lower oven temperature to 350°F for an additional 35-45 minutes of baking. Remove from oven when brown and has pulled away slightly from the sides of the pan. Makes one loaf.
